title: Claude Opus 4.7 Just Dropped... Or Did It Really?

extracted.hook: We just got Opus 4.7, which is apparently Claude's most capable Opus model yet.
transitions: So anyways, uh the real test will be of course when you hop into something like Claude Code and you really start using it for the actual workflows and the the the processes and projects that you are using Claude Code for either way.
Okay, so I had to get that off my chest.
extracted.cta: "that is pretty much my bottom line. 4.7 might be the best AI model ever released up to this point. It might also be the cure to their own disease that Anthropic caused themself. So, either way, you just got to test it out."
analogies: "So, you can talk to Claude in the terminal. Um that way you get some of the extra functionality, too."
curiosity_gaps: "I'm about to drop a full video on how to manage your tokens even better, so that'll be out in like a day or so and definitely want to check that one out"

"There's a bunch of benchmarks, right? And that's the tough thing is you see all this noise. You see all these benchmarks and everything looks like it's better, but you don't truly know until you actually get your hands dirty and play with it."
extracted.vulnerability: "I'm about to get that off my chest."

accelerates: discussing the benchmarks and improvements of Opus 4.7
slows: explaining the issues with Opus 4.6 in detail
